meaning: n.(虚构的富于传奇冒险色彩的)玩偶世界,小人国
examples: North Carolina's Public Interest Research Group released a 2008 Trouble in Toyland report this week at a children's hospital emergency department.
In toyland, LOL Elmo is laughing all the way to the bank, despite being tinged by scandal.
The claims centred around the character of the golliwog, though "generally in Toyland there were more good golliwogs than bad, " said Prof Rudd.
British company Absolute Studios has created an electronic Toyland and computerised versions of Enid Blyton's characters, which have been loved by children for decades.
